item.title: Basil Butler, BP veteran who laid a Shetlands pipeline and smoothed relations with the islanders
item.description: Basil Butler, who has died aged 96, was a veteran oilfield engineer whose career with BP brought him challenging assignments in Alaska, the Middle East and the North Sea.

item.title: Ebola, Palestine and Iran war to top agenda at UN Human Rights Council
item.description: The UN Human Rights Council meets in Geneva on Monday, with issues such as the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and the occupied Palestinian Territories on the agenda. The 47 member states are set to discuss a report on the human rights situation in the Russian-occupied territories of Ukraine, while a debate is also scheduled on Iranian attacks on states in the Gulf.

item.title: Eldest son of Norway’s crown princess sentenced to 4 years in prison for rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was sentenced to four years in prison Monday after being convicted of rape. In addition to the prison sentence, Høiby was ordered to pay compensation to the victims. Høiby, 29, had been charged with sexually assaulting four women who were asleep or otherwise unable to resist between 2018 and 2024.

item.title: Iran says US-Iran deal finalized under Khamenei's leadership
item.description: Iran's Supreme National Security Council said it had finalized the framework agreement with the United States under the leadership of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, according to a statement published by Iranian news agency Tasnim on Monday. The negotiations concluded on Sunday evening, the council said.

item.title: Verdict due in rape trial of Norwegian royal's son Marius Borg Høiby
item.description: A verdict is expected on Monday in the high-profile rape trial of Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit. Høiby faces 40 charges, including four counts of rape, several instances of domestic violence, violations of a restraining order, and drug and traffic offences.

item.title: Ghalibaf: ambitious 'public face' of post-Ali Khamenei Iran
item.description: Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has emerged as the key negotiator and one of the most high-profile figures in the Islamic republic's leadership as it enters a new phase after the US-Israeli war."Following Larijani's assassination, Ghalibaf has emerged as the new public face of the Islamic republic's war effort and diplomacy," said Farzan Sabet, a managing researcher at the Geneva Graduate Institute.

item.title: Russian attack sets fire to centuries-old religious site in Kyiv and kills 5 in Kharkiv
item.description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine killed five rescuers in Kharkiv and wounded at least 20 people in the capital Kyiv on Monday as strikes set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks. The rescuers were killed in Kharkiv by a second Russian strike as they fought a blaze caused by an earlier attack, Interior Minister Ihor Klymenko said. A series of powerful explosions echoed across Kyiv, with a wave of ballistic missiles followed by Shahed drones as many people sought shelter underground and officials urged residents to take cover.

item.title: Ukraine’s historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery on fire following major Russian attack
item.description: A prominent Ukrainian monastery complex in the heart of Kyiv is on fire following a massive Russian attack overnight into Monday that wounded more than a dozen people in the capital, according to local authorities.

item.title: 6 Arrested in Brazil After Woman Dies During Bungee Jump at ‘Skeleton Bridge’

item.title: Zimbabwe’s climate migrants fear eviction as crackdown intensifies
item.description: Many settled in Zimbabwe’s fertile Eastern Highlands after drought left their home areas unable to support farming.

item.title: European leaders ready for mission in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Germany, France, the United Kingdom and Italy have said they are committed to supporting the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz following the announcement of an agreement between the United States and Iran. The urgent reopening of the strait with "unconditional and unrestricted freedom of navigation is essential," German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said in a joint statement.

item.title: Oil and gas supplies could take months to return to normal after Iran deal, energy experts say
item.description: High oil and gasoline prices and energy supply problems won't be solved overnight, despite an agreement to end the Iran war and open the Strait of Hormuz announced Sunday. The slow pace of the process of shipping and refining crude oil, and doubts about the security of traveling through the strait mean the effect won't be seen immediately, they said. Ships loaded with crude oil have been stranded in the Persian Gulf for more than three months, unable to safely travel through the waterway, through which about a fifth of the world’s oil and gasoline supplies typically traveled before the war began.

item.title: American singer Oliver Tree listed aboard helicopter involved in fatal midair collision in Brazil

item.title: Berlin air show ILA closes with record attendance of 110,000 visitors

item.title: G7 protest turns from carnival to violent stand-off

item.description: A magnitude-5. 1 earthquake struck the south-west of the Greek Peloponnese peninsula on Sunday evening, with no damage reported so far, according to the Geodynamic Institute of the University of Athens.

The IDF killed senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq in a precision strike in southern Lebanon, the military announced on Sunday.

item.title: US singer Oliver Tree aboard helicopter in deadly mid-air crash in Brazil
item.description: American alt-pop singer and internet personality Oliver Tree was aboard one of two helicopters that collided Sunday mid-air in Brazil, leaving all six people aboard the aircraft dead, a police source told AFP. The source shared the manifest of each plane and said the victims could not be formally identified yet as they were badly burned in the crash.Small aircraft crashes are common in Brazil, the world's fifth-largest country.

item.title: Britain expected to set out under 16s social media restrictions
item.description: Britain is expected to set out restrictions on social media for children under 16 on Monday that could include banning popular platforms and features ‌that are deemed too addictive for the wellbeing of young people online. Britain has increasingly toughened its approach ‌to tech companies in recent years, urging or forcing them to impose age verification, adapt their algorithms and, most recently, prevent children from circulating ​nude images taken on mobile phones. But with a growing awareness of the mental health risks posed by children spending too much time online, Prime Minister Keir Starmer has decided to go further after speaking to parents and considering evidence from Australia, which brought in a ban for under 16s last year.

item.title: Pakistan PM says US and Iran reach peace deal after talks
item.description: June 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said early on Monday that the United States and Iran have reached a peace deal.

item.title: Ebola cases in eastern Congo climb to 782 and deaths reach 181, authorities say
item.description: The number of confirmed Ebola cases in Congo has increased to 782, with 181 deaths, the Congolese Ministry of Health said in a statement on social platform X on Sunday evening. The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved vaccine or treatment, unlike the “Zaire virus,” which was responsible for most of Congo’s past 16 outbreaks of the disease. The outbreak is concentrated in Congo’s eastern province of Ituri, which accounts for more than 90% of the cases.

item.title: Police officers attempt to disperse haredi protest in Jerusalem
item.description: "Dozens of rioters began blocking traffic routes and public buses with their bodies, significantly disrupting the routine of life in the area and putting road users at risk," the police said. Haredi (ultra-Orthodox) activists protested on Jerusalem's Bar Ilan Street on Sunday, Israel Police confirmed.

item.description: Brazil's air force has launched an investigation into the incident, which took place over Rio de Janeiro.

item.description: A woman was left in critical condition after she was bitten by a shark at a beach in Sydney, Australia, and it's not the first, or even second, time it's happened this month.

item.description: South Africa has repatriated 2,745 foreigners in the week after President Cyril Ramaphosa vowed tougher action against illegal immigration, the country's home affairs minister said on Sunday."As of last night, the number we can report is 2,745 repatriations that have come in this period since the president spoke," Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber told reporters.

item.description: AJ probe: Israel expands military control by 1,000sq km on three fronts, using land grabs to mask strategic failures.

item.title: Bitter communion: Cuban priests ordered to ration mass wafers
item.description: To the never-ending list of scarcities in Cuba comes a new addition: communion for the Catholic faithful.On exiting the church to find the power out again in the neighborhood, they vowed to keep the faith.

item.title: Helicopters collide over Rio de Janeiro, killing 6
item.description: Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people aboard, firefighters said. Rio de Janeiro’s Military Fire Department said that one of the helicopters crashed on the parking lot of a car dealership, where several electric vehicles were parked, igniting a fire that was extinguished. Police said that American singer and comedian Oliver Tree was on the list of passengers handed to aviation authorities, but have not been able to identify the bodies of those killed in the crash.

item.title: Hezbollah continues attacks on north Israel after Beirut suburbs hit
item.description: Lebanon's Iranian-backed Hezbollah militia has continued its attacks on northern Israel even after Israeli strikes hit the suburbs of Beirut, the Israeli military said on Sunday evening. A projectile fired from Lebanon struck near the border town of Kiryat Shmona, the Israeli military said.

item.title: Families of imprisoned Iranian protesters targeted in fraud scam, Iranian legal group claims
item.description: Many family members are facing extreme financial hardship to raise the funds requested by scammers in exchange for legal help, some going as far as to sell their houses. Dozens of families of those imprisoned by the Islamic regime for participating in the December demonstrations have been targeted by a series of costly fraud schemes, Dadban, a network of pro-bono lawyers and jurists that provides legal counsel, education, and support to political prisoners, protesters, and rights activists in Ir

item.title: Armenia's elections regulator confirms victory for PM's party in poll closely watched by Russia
item.description: Armenia’s polling authority on Sunday confirmed that the party led by Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan won a general election seen as a vote on its geopolitical future and a test of Russia’s influence in the South Caucasus country. Pashinyan’s government is seeking closer relations with the European Union and the United States despite longstanding ties with Russia that have been championed by his critics. Final results issued by the Central Electoral Commission for the ballot held on June 7 showed the ruling Civil Contract party won 49.7% of the vote, and it will be able to form a government.

item.title: IDF soldier ‘just wanted to kill’ my son
item.description: A seven-month-old baby was shot in the head with an armour-piercing bullet by an Israeli soldier “who just wanted to kill”, his father has said.

item.title: Woman Dies After Falling From Bridge During Bungee Jump Accident in Brazil
item.description: A young woman in Brazil lost her life during a bungee-jumping excursion after authorities say critical safety procedures may have been missed before the jump. Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas, 21, died Saturday after plunging from a bridge in Limeira, a city in São Paulo state, according to multiple Brazilian media reports. 🔥🚨LATEST: Footage has released of the moment the 21-year-old woman was accidentally killed when Entre Cordas workers forgot to attach her safety rope and and threw her off the the “Skeleton Bridge” in Limeira, a city in Brazil’s São Paulo state.

item.title: In crisis-hit Cuba, World Cup offers brief respite
item.description: "You need to be happy," reads graffiti spray-painted on a ruined building swamped in trash in Cuba's capital Havana.There was no such luck for Ismael Veranes, the round-faced human resources director of Cuba's national theater.

item.title: Baltic States mark 85th anniversary of Soviet mass deportations
item.description: The Baltic States marked the 85th anniversary on Sunday of the mass deportation of tens of thousands of people by the Soviet Union with nationwide commemorative events. On June 14, 1941, and the days that followed, a first wave of deportations saw more than 10,000 Estonians, more than 15,000 Latvians and nearly 18,000 Lithuanians taken to Siberia from the states annexed by the Soviet Union during World War II.

item.title: Disabled oil tanker received dozens of warnings before US opened fire, AP source says
item.description: The tanker disabled by the American military while trying to run the U.S. blockade of Iran had dismissed nearly 60 verbal warnings as well as other shows of force before being fired upon, a U.S. official told The Associated Press. The ship’s crew also ignored eight shows of force by military aircraft that included fired flares and flyovers, as well as two final warnings before being fired upon Wednesday, according to the official, who was not authorized to publicly discuss the sensitive matter and spoke on condition of anonymity.

item.title: Justin Trudeau skipped Canada World Cup opener because girlfriend Katy Perry performed at US game
item.description: Former Canada Prime Minister Justin Trudeau attended the United States' World Cup opener against Paraguay on Friday at Inglewood, California, rather than his own nation's game vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina in Toronto. Trudeau said he made the decision because his girlfriend, Katy Perry, performed in the pregame show before the match at SoFi Stadium. Trudeau was Canada's prime minister from 2015-25.

item.title: German minister ‘used AI to write Holocaust speech’
item.description: A German minister has been criticised for allegedly using AI to write a Holocaust remembrance speech.

item.title: Rome’s ‘wedding cake’ monument sinking into ground
item.description: An Italian landmark built to celebrate Italy’s “democratic and European values” is slowly sinking into the ground.

item.title: ‘Spider-Man of Yemen’ dies in fall while climbing volcanic crater
item.description: A daredevil adventurer known as “The Spider-Man of Yemen” has died after falling into a volcano crater while attempting to climb vertical rock faces without safety equipment, authorities said. Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar, 30, was climbing the steep walls of the Hardah Dam volcanic crater in the southern province of Dhale on Friday when he lost his grip and fell into the 120 meter (393 feet) crater, according to the Civil Defense Authority, which posted a short video capturing the moment of his fall.

item.title: ‘Unacceptable’: London protesters slam illegal Israeli settlement land sale
item.description: Demonstrators denounce event promoting Israeli settlement land and property sales as a violation of international law.

item.title: Iran questioning deal with US after Israeli strikes in Lebanon
item.description: Iran has called into question a pending framework agreement with the United States to end the war after Israel attacked the suburbs of the Lebanese capital Beirut, prompting criticism from US President Donald Trump. "The Zionists' incursion into Dahiyeh has once again shown that America either lacks the will to fulfil its commitments or the ability to do so," Iran's parliamentary speaker and chief negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, wrote on X on Sunday.

item.description: The Israeli military launched an attack on Hezbollah targets in Beirut on Sunday, accusing the group of violating a cease-fire agreement.

item.description: British forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned oil tanker belonging to Russia's shadow fleet in a dramatic night-time operation hailed by Kyiv as a blow to Moscow’s war machine.In March, the British government announced that UK forces would be able to board and seize shadow fleet vessels passing through its waters.

item.title: Brazil Police Confirm Arrests After Woman Dies in Bungee Jump Fall

item.description: Iran has called into question a pending framework agreement with the United States to end the war after Israel once again attacked the suburbs of the Lebanese capital Beirut. item.title: Moscow: Russian troops advancing on Kostiantynivka

item.title: Palestinian death toll in Gaza tops 73,000, officials say, as Israel strikes despite ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Palestinian death toll from the Israel-Hamas war has surpassed 73,000, Gaza’s Health Ministry said Sunday, despite a fragile ceasefire that has been described as stalled. Israel has continued to strike inside the territory despite the deal reached in October, saying it carries out strikes against Hamas and other militants who pose a threat, and in response to ceasefire violations, including occasional attacks. Confirmation of the latest Palestinian toll came from Zaher al-Waheidi, head of the ministry’s records department, and Hamza Salem from the ministry’s public relations department.

item.title: China direct strike threat to Australia 'growing': report
item.description: China is capable of a direct missile strike on Australia and the threat is growing as Beijing amasses long-range and hypersonic weapons and builds islands in the South China Sea, an Australian think tank said on Sunday.The Dong Feng-26 intermediate-range ballistic missile could reach northern Australia if deployed from one of Beijing's artificially built islands in the South China Sea, it said.

item.title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
item.description: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Beirut on Sunday targeting Hezbollah infrastructure, despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war. The strikes threatened to hamper negotiations over a deal, which in its current form is a deep disappointment to Israel's government. The last time Israel struck the Beirut suburbs a week ago, it set off the most serious escalation of fighting between Iran and Israel since the tenuous ceasefire took hold April 7.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill 1 and spark fire at oil facility in Russia
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack killed one person in Russia’s southwestern Oryol region, local officials said Sunday, while a separate strike hit an oil facility as part of Kyiv’s campaign of strikes on military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Oryol regional Gov. Andrei Klychkov said Sunday that one person died and nine were wounded when a Ukrainian drone struck a residential building overnight in the regional capital, also called Oryol. Local authorities in Russia’s Yaroslavl region, around 700 kilometers (440 miles) from the Ukrainian border, said fuel storage facilities caught fire after being hit by a drone.

item.title: Ukraine’s AI drones entered ‘Terminator mode’ and killed Russian soldiers — no human fired
item.description: A Ukrainian drone maker confirms the first battlefield kill by fully autonomous AI drones, marking a historic threshold in modern warfare.

item.title: Cyberattack disrupts banking services in Iran
item.description: Electronic banking services in Iran were severely disrupted on Saturday after a cyberattack targeted several state-owned banks, temporarily preventing online payments. According to a report by the Iranian business news outlet Eghtesad News on Saturday evening, the country's Banking Coordination Council confirmed the incidents but said the disruptions had since been resolved and online transactions were functioning normally again.

item.title: Young climber dies in accident in southern Germany
item.description: A 22-year-old climber has died after falling approximately 130 metres in a mountain accident in southern Germany, police said on Sunday. The incident occurred on Saturday in the Karwendel mountains near Mittenwald, close to the border with Austria.

item.description: James Boyard's abduction is the highest-ranking abduction in the violence-wracked country in recent years, according to reports.

item.description: A Labour MP has hit out at “neo-colonial” animal rights activists after they launched a campaign to ban gannet hunting on Scotland’s Isle of Lewis.

item.title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia’s shadow fleet
item.description: Britain is investigating a sanctioned tanker that is suspected of being part of the Russian “shadow fleet,” shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow’s war on Ukraine, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday. British armed forces boarded and detained the vessel, the Smyrtos, on Sunday in the English Channel, in what the country’s Defense Ministry called “the first UK-led operation of its kind.” The vessel will be held and monitored off the south coast of England for investigation, according to a statement by the Defense Ministry.

item.title: Israel reports Hezbollah drone attack in northern border area
item.description: The Lebanese Hezbollah militia has launched another drone attack on northern Israel, Israeli authorities said on Sunday. The Israeli military said two impacts were recorded on Israeli territory near the border with Lebanon, but no injuries were reported.

item.description: The Berlin-Hamburg railway line reopened on Sunday after over 10 months of major renovation works, rail operator Deutsche Bahn said. The first long-distance passenger train departed Hamburg central station at 5:36 am (0336 GMT), with two minutes' delay.

item.description: Trump says a deal to stop the Iran war could be signed as early as Sunday, but Tehran downplays his optimism.

item.title: Taiwan’s spy agency launches webpage for Chinese nationals to report tips
item.description: Taiwan’s intelligence agency said on Sunday it is establishing an information-reporting channel for Chinese nationals to offer tips securely, at a time when tensions between Beijing and the self-ruled island remain elevated. In a statement, Taiwan’s National Security Bureau said they are launching a webpage that will act as a secure channel for Chinese nationals to provide intelligence-related information, saying that an increasing number of people have recently approached relevant agencies in Taiwan wishing to “provide various types of information.” “In recent years, China’s economy has faced mounting difficulties, while political control has remained tight,” the statement said.

item.description: The Berlin-Hamburg railway line is to reopen on Sunday after around 10 and a half months of major renovation works, rail operator Deutsche Bahn said. item.description: The Zaporizhzhya nuclear power plant in south-eastern Ukraine, which is occupied by Russian troops, has been reconnected to the electricity grid after nearly three days offline, both the Moscow-installed plant management and Ukrainian nuclear operator Energoatom posted on Telegram. A damaged high-voltage line had been repaired, both said on Saturday.

item.description: Rival protesters clashed in Glasgow on Saturday after thousands descended on the city for an anti-racism rally. Some in the small counter-protest group performed what appeared to be Nazi salutes as the two groups gathered at Buchanan Street in the city centre.

item.title: Police dress up as World Cup mascots to carry out drug sting
item.description: The officers in mascot costumes used a metal sledgehammer to break down a door to enter with colleagues.

item.title: King Charles Leads Slimmed-Down Royal Balcony at Trooping the Colour 2026
item.description: King Charles returned to the Buckingham Palace balcony on Saturday for the traditional close of Trooping the Colour, the annual parade marking the British monarch’s ... Read More

item.title: Syria arrests 235 ISIS operatives in three months, foils seven terror plots
item.description: ISIS remains a threat to Syria, increasingly relying on small-scale attacks, covert operations, and decentralized cells to target security forces, public facilities, and local communities. Syria’s Interior Ministry announced on Monday that security forces had arrested 235 members of the Islamic State group (ISIS) over the past three months as part of a broad security campaign aimed at dismantling the group’s active cells and preventing attacks intended to destabilize the country.

item.title: China's Dalian Airport Isn't A Building - It's An Artificial Island
item.description: China is currently undertaking one of the most ambitious airport projects ever seen in an effort to expand its nationwide air transport footprint.

item.title: Three in custody after major explosion in Amsterdam injures seven
item.description: Police in Amsterdam are investigating whether criminals caused an explosion at an apartment building that injured seven people and forced 400 residents to evacuate. Three people have been detained, police said on Saturday.

item.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting that followed stabbing
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in Belfast on Saturday to denounce anti-immigrant rioters who set fire to homes and cars earlier in the week after a brutal stabbing blamed on an asylum seeker. The anti-racism rally came after nights of fiery violence in parts of Northern Ireland following the arrest of a 30-year-old man from Sudan on charges of attempted murder in a stabbing that left a man partly blind. More than two dozen people were left homeless and 12 police officers were injured in what officials called “thuggery.”

item.title: Peru police disguised as World Cup mascots arrest a suspected drug dealer in Lima
item.description: Two Peruvian police officers disguised as World Cup mascots Clutch and Maple helped to arrest a suspected drug dealer in Lima. Colonel Carlos Alcántara, head of the Green Squadron —a unit that combats common crime — said they captured Carlos Cabrera, 48, with the help of the two undercover agents on Thursday during the opening match of the World Cup between Mexico and South Africa. “Thanks to intelligence work, we realized that this person was a diehard football fan and was caught up in World Cup fever," Alcántara said.

item.title: Pakistan says US-Iran agreement to be signed tomorrow
item.description: Pakistan, which has been acting as a mediator between the United States and Iran, said on Saturday that an electronic signing ceremony for a peace agreement is expected to take place on Sunday. In a statement posted on X, Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said that during a telephone conversation, the Saudi and Pakistani foreign ministers "welcomed the US–Iran negotiations in their final stage, with the electronic signing ceremony scheduled for tomorrow.

item.title: Woman pulled from sea after shark attack in Sydney
item.description: A woman has suffered critical injuries after being bitten by a shark “as big as a car” off a beach in Sydney.

item.title: Starmer tells Rutte UK will publish defence plan before NATO July summit
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Gaza post-‘ceasefire’ deaths hit 983 as Israeli attack targets refugee camp
item.description: Israeli attack kills one person in central Gaza’s Bureij camp, as a disabled Palestinian is shot in occupied West Bank.

item.title: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
item.description: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar's death highlights continuing security challenges facing parts of the country.

item.title: Mass shootings in South Africa’s poorest areas are a symptom of organized crime and police failures
item.description: A mass shooting in South Africa this week was the latest in a trend of killings in some of the country's poorest areas that experts say is a symptom of organized crime gangs taking advantage of the failures and sometimes absence of police. The shooting carried out by multiple suspects in an informal settlement of shacks in Johannesburg left 12 people dead and at least 15 wounded, according to authorities. Alongside that, South Africa's police force has been embroiled in allegations of corruption and collusion with criminal syndicates.

item.title: As Iran and US near a deal, Tehran remembers another recent bloody conflict
item.description: Iranian authorities say assassinations and strikes over the past year have failed to deter them.

item.title: Iran plans to hold funeral of slain ayatollah Ali Khamenei in July
item.description: More than 100 days after Iran’s supreme leader ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in Tehran during a targeted Israeli strike, authorities have announced that his funeral will take place during Muharram, the Islamic month of mourning. The official funeral ceremony is scheduled for July 9 in Khamenei’s home city of Mashhad, a major religious centre in north-eastern Iran.

item.title: Trump: Tren de Aragua leader Guerrero Flores killed in joint strike
item.description: An alleged Tren de Aragua transnational gang leader was killed in Venezuela, said U.S. President Donald Trump and Venezuelan officials.

item.title: Woman seriously injured in shark attack at Sydney beach
item.description: The woman, 35, was pulled from the water by members of the public and then airlifted to hospital, police say.

item.title: Woman Critically Injured in Shark Attack: 'There Was a Huge Amount of Blood in the Water,' Eyewitness Says
item.description: The woman suffered significant injuries to her left lower leg and upper arms

item.title: Autonomous Robots Confirmed to Have Killed Human Soldiers
item.description: "We just launch it and we know everything will be dead — everything that will be found there in this particular area will be dead."

item.title: At least 17 people killed by gunmen in northwestern Nigeria
item.description: Gunmen killed at least 17 farmers and wounded at least 13 others as they worked in their fields in northwestern Nigeria, a local official and a resident said. “The farmers were working on their lands when the bandits suddenly attacked and killed 17 of them,” Shehu Musa, a resident of Maradun, told The Associated Press on Saturday, adding that the wounded were being treated at a hospital. The attack was a result of the refusal of the Zamfara state government to negotiate with the gunmen, Sanusi Dosara, chairman of the Maradun local government, said in a statement.

item.title: Thousands gather in London to celebrate Trooping the Colour
item.description: Thousands gathered in London for King Charles III's Trooping the Colour birthday parade while anti-monarchy protesters held up pictures of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

item.title: Protesters tear down Albanian development site fences, amid anger over coastal projects
item.description: RRJOLL, Albania, June 13 () - Some 200 protesters on Saturday tore down metal and razor wire fences surrounding a ‌luxury development site on Albania's Adriatic coast, in another ‌sign of growing anger against construction in environmentally sensitive areas. Albanians have been protesting ​for weeks against a planned luxury resort backed by a company linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.

item.title: New museum quarter opens at Berlin's main train station
item.description: A new museum quarter has opened in the area around Berlin's Central Railway Station, or Hauptbahnhof. The Museum of Natural History, the Hamburger Bahnhof contemporary art gallery, the Futurium and the Charité Museum of the History of Medicine have joined forces to form the MuseumsMeileMitte, or Museum Mile in the Mitte district.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Threaten World Cup After Alleged FBI Drone Breach
item.description: Iran-linked group Handala threatens the 2026 World Cup, claiming it hacked FBI surveillance drones. SITE disputes the proof.

item.title: Free compost amid changes to garden waste recycling
item.description: The States of Guernsey says it is using windrow composting to offer residents a "superior" product.

item.title: Thousands attend anti-racism rally in Belfast
item.description: Thousands of people have attended an anti-racism rally in Belfast following days of unrest provoked by a knife attack. Organized by the Unite Against Racism organization, the Together Against Hate rally is taking place outside Belfast City Hall.

item.title: Suspected rabid jackals attack campers, leaving 11 injured including child bitten on face
item.description: "My daughter Alice screamed, ‘It hurts! It hurts!’ I quickly turned on the light, and her entire face was covered in blood. I was terrified,” said Elena, the mother of a child injured in the attack.

item.title: King and Queen cheered by crowds at Trooping the Colour
item.description: The annual event marks the King's official birthday and features more than 1,400 members of the armed forces.

item.title: 2 Young Cousins Dead and 3 Other Children Injured After Carriage Driven by 7-Year-Old Rolls Over Multiple Times
item.description: “Our hearts go out to everyone who was impacted by this unthinkable event," said B.C. RCMP Staff Sgt. Kris Clark of the fatal crash on June 4

item.title: Lifeguard helps save woman critically injured in Australia shark attack
item.description: Lifeguard Charlie Verco said he was on his paddleboard when he saw the 11-foot shark emerge and attack a swimmer.

item.title: UAE ‘to hand Iran billions of dollars in exchange for halting strikes’
item.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) agreed to release billions of dollars to Iran in exchange for Tehran halting its attacks on the Gulf state, according to reports.

item.title: Russian MP warns Putin: We’re on the brink of social collapse
item.description: A Russian MP has demanded that Vladimir Putin deliver a plan to end the war as he berated the Kremlin’s “ineffective leadership”.

item.title: UNRWA fires 70 Gaza staffers amid allegations of Hamas ties, says terminations not admission of guilt
item.description: UNRWA fires 70 Gaza staff members amid long-standing Israeli claims the agency collaborates with Hamas, though it says dismissals are not an admission.

item.title: Funeral for Iran's supreme leader set for July, while a deal nears to end the war
item.description: Funeral ceremonies for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei will be in July, the country's state-run television said Saturday, as mediators said an agreement to end the war was close. The funeral, burial and farewell events for Khamenei will take place between July 4 and 9 during Muharram, a traditional period of mourning in the Shiite Muslim calendar. Khamenei was killed in the opening attack of the war that Israel and the United States launched against Iran in late February.

item.title: Art exhibition opens at presidential palace in Berlin
item.description: Visitors can spend the next two weeks exploring the rooms of Bellevue Palace, the official residence of Germany's president in Berlin ahead of a major refurbishment of the building. Among the attractions is a pop-up exhibition of contemporary art entitled "Freiraum Kunst" (Free Space Art), which opened to the public on Saturday morning.

item.title: Iran announces funeral, burial dates for late Supreme Leader Khamenei
item.description: Funeral ceremonies will take place in Tehran and Qom before a burial in his home town of Mashhad.

item.title: Ukraine's defense firms split themselves up to avoid being a big target. Europe now needs to do the same, they say.
item.description: Ukrainian defense firms work across multiple sites to avoid being big targets for Russia. They say European counterparts need to start doing the same.

item.title: King Charles Honors Charlotte Tilbury, Sandra Choi, and Clare Hornby in 2026 Birthday List
item.description: The British monarch also recognized David Emanuel, who codesigned the late Princess Diana's wedding dress with his former wife Elizabeth more than four decades ago.

item.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal could be finalized within next 24 hours
item.description: Pakistan, which has been mediating in the conflict between the United States and Iran, said on Saturday that the finalization of a peace agreement is expected within the next 24 hours, with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif saying the two sides are "closer to a peace deal than ever before. " In a post on X, Sharif wrote that Pakistan is preparing for the electronic signing of the agreement, which should take place after the deal is finalized.
